intended_use.purpose: Healthcare encounter management system enabling practitioners to manage patient appointments, consultations, medical records, prescriptions, referrals, and handovers within a digital health platform
intended_use.medical_indication: General healthcare - supports primary care consultations, follow-up appointments, asynchronous consultations, and care coordination across practitioners and care units
intended_use.health_function: Manages the complete lifecycle of patient healthcare encounters including appointment scheduling, clinical documentation (notes, diagnoses, prescriptions, referrals), practitioner-patient communication, handover of care between practitioners, integration with external health systems, and billing coordination
intended_use.clinical_workflow: Digital healthcare delivery workflow: patient booking → triage/interview → practitioner assignment → consultation (video/async/scheduled) → clinical documentation → prescription/referral generation → follow-up scheduling → care handover (if needed) → billing/payment → episode closure
claims: Manages patient appointments across multiple modalities (video consultation, asynchronous consultation, scheduled appointments)
Enables secure handover of patient encounters between healthcare practitioners
Supports clinical documentation including notes, diagnoses, prescriptions, and referrals
Integrates with external health systems (Alfa e-Recept for prescriptions, TakeCare for case notes, Efrikort for patient cards)
Provides practitioner notification and alert system for care coordination
Tracks patient medical history and medication records
Supports waiting room management and appointment queuing
Enables secure video meetings and messaging between practitioners and patients
Software Safety Classification: Class A (highest safety level per catalog-info.yaml)
contraindications: Not for emergency medical situations requiring immediate care
Not a standalone diagnostic system - clinical judgment required
Cross-care-provider handovers disabled by default (requires explicit feature flag)
Not for use without valid practitioner credentials and active shift

source.evidence: Software safety classification: A (highest level), Manages appointments, consultations, prescriptions, referrals, and handovers

intended_users.description: Licensed healthcare practitioners (doctors, nurses, specialists) providing remote consultations and managing patient encounters
required_training: GDPR and patient data protection compliance
Handover procedures and care coordination protocols

intended_users.description: Patients seeking remote healthcare services and managing their own appointments
required_skills: Ability to describe symptoms and medical history

intended_users.description: Care unit administrators, secretaries, and support staff managing schedules, billing, and operational tasks
System administrators and DevOps engineers maintaining the service infrastructure

restrictions: Practitioners must have valid medical credentials verified via Directory service
Practitioners must have active shift to accept appointments
Patients can only access their own appointments (or related minors)
Cross-care-provider operations require explicit feature flag authorization
Limited access roles (ROLE_PRACTITIONER_LIMITED_ACCESS) restricted to specific patient sets

source.evidence: Role-based access control with ROLE_PRACTITIONER, ROLE_PATIENT, ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_SYSTEM, ROLE_SECRETARY; @PreAuthorize annotations; shift validation requirements

browsers: Modern browsers supporting WebRTC for video consultations
platforms.hardware_requirements: Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources for production workload (10-100 database connections per instance)

network_requirements.latency: 30 second database query timeout, 10 second remote service connect timeout, 5 minute read timeout

integration_dependencies.purpose: Practitioner and patient identity verification, organizational structure
Video consultation rooms and patient-practitioner messaging
Patient triage questionnaires and medical history collection
Push notifications and SMS alerts to practitioners and patients
Task management and action items for practitioners
Electronic prescription submission to Swedish national prescription system
Integration with Swedish regional health records (journalsystem)

interface_requirements.description: RESTful HTTP API providing CRUD operations for appointments, consultations, prescriptions, referrals, and clinical notes
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: RESTful HTTP with JSON payloads, versioned endpoints (v1, v2, v3), role-based access control via JWT authentication
accessibility: Error messages with HTTP status codes

interface_requirements.description: JMS messaging interface for event-driven communication with external systems
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: Publish-subscribe messaging via JMS topics for appointment events, handover events, consultation events, payment events, referral events, and practitioner online status

interface_requirements.description: Security interface requiring JWT-based authentication with role-based authorization
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: JWT bearer token authentication with claims for user identity, roles, patient relationships, and care unit associations
input_methods: HTTP Authorization header with Bearer token
accessibility: Token validation errors with clear messages

interface_requirements.description: Database query interface with QueryDSL predicate-based filtering and pagination
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: Dynamic query construction via QueryDSL predicates, pageable result sets, repository pattern with JPA

interface_requirements.description: Internationalization (i18n) interface supporting multiple languages via translation keys
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: Translation service resolving language-specific message keys for handover notifications, error messages, and clinical terms
interface_requirements.display_requirements: Localized messages based on practitioner/patient locale

interface_requirements.description: Monitoring and observability interface via Spring Boot Actuator and Prometheus metrics
interface_requirements.interaction_pattern: Health check endpoints, application metrics, heap dumps, thread dumps, Flyway migration status

immunity_requirements.threat: Resource exhaustion from database connection leaks
immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Hikari connection pool with min 10, max 100 connections, 5-second connection timeout

immunity_requirements.threat: Runaway database queries causing system slowdown
immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Global query timeout of 30 seconds enforced at JPA level

immunity_requirements.threat: External service unavailability causing cascading failures
immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Remote client timeouts: 10 second connect timeout, 5 minute read timeout

immunity_requirements.threat: Memory exhaustion from unbounded cache growth
immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Caffeine caches with explicit maxSize and expireAfterWrite TTL per cache
JPA optimistic locking via @Version fields, database UNIQUE constraints
immunity_requirements.implementation: version column in appointment_handover_process, UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id

immunity_requirements.threat: Malicious input causing SQL injection or XSS
immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Parameterized queries via JPA/Hibernate, input validation via @Valid annotations, safe character filtering
immunity_requirements.implementation: safeCharacters.alphanumeric and safeCharacters.special configuration, JPA prepared statements

immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: Immutable audit events (AppointmentHandoverEvent), append-only event table with FK constraints
immunity_requirements.implementation: appointment_handover_event table with FK to appointment, no update/delete operations

immunity_requirements.protection_mechanism: OWASP Dependency Check Maven plugin scanning dependencies
immunity_requirements.implementation: org.owasp:dependency-check-maven plugin in pom.xml build lifecycle

permissions: Read appointment (requires ownership or patient relationship)
Update appointment (requires active shift and authorization)
Access patient health profile (requires authorization check)
View clinical notes (requires care unit authorization)

authentication.session_management: Stateless JWT-based authentication with token validation on each request
authentication.password_policy: Not managed by this service - delegated to identity provider

checksum_algorithms: Not explicitly implemented at data field level

communication_protection.tls_version: Not explicitly configured - relies on platform/infrastructure
communication_protection.encryption_at_rest: Not implemented at application level - delegated to database and infrastructure

files: TakeCare x509 configuration for certificate-based communication

input_validation: SQL injection protection via parameterized queries
Field length limits preventing buffer overflows

privacy_requirements.data_type: Patient personal identifiers (personnummer, name, contact info)
privacy_requirements.protection_mechanism: Role-based access control, patient relationship validation, audit logging
privacy_requirements.legal_basis: Consent and legitimate interest (healthcare provision) under GDPR and Swedish Patientdatalagen
privacy_requirements.retention_period: As per care unit policy and Swedish healthcare regulations

privacy_requirements.data_type: Clinical health data (diagnoses, prescriptions, notes, medical history)
privacy_requirements.protection_mechanism: Database secrecy classification (C), sensitivity level 2, encryption in transit, access control
privacy_requirements.legal_basis: Consent and legal obligation (healthcare provision) under GDPR Article 9 (special category data)
privacy_requirements.retention_period: Swedish healthcare record retention requirements (typically 10 years after last contact)

privacy_requirements.protection_mechanism: JWT-based authentication, role verification, shift validation
privacy_requirements.retention_period: As per employment and regulatory requirements

privacy_requirements.protection_mechanism: Database encryption, access control, audit logging, 1024 character limit
External payment service integration, no card details stored locally, PCI-DSS compliance via Klarna
Feature flag ENABLE_HANDOVERS_ACROSS_CARE_PROVIDERS (disabled by default), care provider boundary validation
privacy_requirements.legal_basis: Explicit consent required for cross-organizational sharing

upgrade_support.data_migration: Flyway database migrations with versioned schema changes, baseline-on-migrate enabled for legacy databases
upgrade_support.backward_compatibility: API versioning (v1, v2, v3) allows gradual migration, deprecated endpoints marked in OpenAPI docs

rollback_support.mechanism: Kubernetes deployment rollback, database rollback via Flyway (manual process)
rollback_support.data_handling: Database schema rollback requires manual intervention, data loss risk for non-reversible migrations

files: Application containerization implies k8s rollback capability

security_updates.delivery_mechanism: Maven dependency updates, container image rebuilds, Kubernetes rolling deployments
security_updates.frequency: Continuous monitoring via OWASP Dependency Check, updates as vulnerabilities discovered
security_updates.notification: GitLab CI/CD pipeline failures on dependency check issues

distribution_integrity.secure_delivery: GitLab CI/CD pipeline builds container images, pushes to registry, Helm deploys to Kubernetes

data_deletion.method: SystemDataErasureController provides partner data export/delete functionality

data_retention.duration: Typically 10 years after last patient contact, varies by care unit policy

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Protection of personal health data as special category data (Article 9)
regulatory_requirements.implementation: Role-based access control, audit logging, data export/deletion capabilities, consent management

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Legal basis for processing patient health records, access control, audit trails
regulatory_requirements.implementation: Audit logging of all patient data access, practitioner credential verification, shift validation
regulatory_requirements.evidence: @Audit(throttle = Throttle.HOURLY) on sensitive endpoints, shift validation before access

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Integration with national e-prescription system (Alfa e-Recept)
regulatory_requirements.implementation: Alfa E-Recept client integration for prescription submission
regulatory_requirements.evidence: Alfa E-Recept configuration in application.yml, alfa-erecept.client dependency

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Software as a Medical Device classification and risk management
regulatory_requirements.implementation: Software Safety Classification: A (highest level), risk analysis documented, traceability to requirements

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Quality management system, risk management, software lifecycle processes
regulatory_requirements.implementation: Documented software requirements, architecture, risk analysis, and verification per IEC 62304 structure

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Integration with regional health information exchanges (TakeCare)
regulatory_requirements.implementation: TakeCare Xchange integration with x509 certificate-based authentication for case note export
regulatory_requirements.evidence: takecare.xchange configuration in application.yml with x509 keystore/truststore

gaps.description: OpenAPI documentation exists but complete API specification document not found in repository
gaps.recommendation: Generate and review complete OpenAPI specification from code annotations

gaps.description: Immunity requirements are implemented but formal verification test evidence not located
gaps.recommendation: Create integration tests demonstrating resource exhaustion protection, timeout enforcement, and resilience patterns

gaps.description: TLS version requirements not explicitly configured in application
gaps.recommendation: Document minimum TLS version requirement in deployment configuration and verify infrastructure enforcement

gaps.description: Data retention policy not explicitly defined in application code
gaps.recommendation: Document retention policy per data type and implement automated retention enforcement or manual review procedures

gaps.description: Container image signing and checksum verification not evident in CI/CD pipeline
gaps.recommendation: Implement container image signing in GitLab CI and verify signatures during deployment

gaps.description: No dedicated README or product documentation describing intended use in user-facing language
gaps.recommendation: Create user-facing documentation clearly stating intended use, contraindications, and user instructions per IEC 82304-1 clause 8 (accompanying documents)

to_system_requirements.use_requirement: User profile: Healthcare practitioners with credentials
Privacy: GDPR and patient data protection

software_requirements: Prescription creation and Alfa e-Recept integration
